summary:
The patient’s symptoms indicate an imbalance between air and bone conducted sounds, ruled out by normal Rinne testing while Weber being centered hints at perilymphatic leakage causing fluctuating hearing levels especially noticeable in noise; thus,
The most probable cause appears to be \boxed{\textbf{a Perilymph Fistula}}. This accounts for her specific pattern of auditory function changes across varying acoustic conditions. Given this analysis focusing directly on critical diagnostic clues from tests performed and symptomatology presented, no other option matches these findings precisely enough without additional supporting data or imaging studies typically required further confirmation steps not available within current scope. Therefore concluding based strictly upon stated facts alone leads us confidently toward identifying perilymph fistulas as primary etiology behind observed phenomena noted herein regarding audiological
